_note: Schema-by-example for the knowledge_answer template. Every field except `topic` and `tldr` is optional — omit any block that the sourced material cannot fill honestly (drop-if-unsourced, per 000-guardrails.mdc). Do NOT invent URLs, dates, or page IDs to fill this file; the K1-K2 knowledge search must supply them.
question: What version of DBR (Databricks Runtime) does AIR currently support?
tldr: Current AIR 3.x runs on DBR 17.3-LTS. Each AIR major version maps to exactly one DBR LTS line.

facts.text: Each AIR major version pins to exactly one DBR LTS — Abacus does not adopt every DBR release (e.g. no AIR based on DBR 16).
New feature work happens on the latest AIR line only. Older AIR versions receive hotfixes for urgent bugs only.
The DBR version is encoded in the Databricks cluster-policy tag, e.g. AIR:3.0.16 - 17.3.x-scala2.13 - Pipeline.
⚠️ Unverified: whether an older AIR line still maps to DBR 11.3-LTS — one prior answer included it, another did not. Confirm live before relying on it.

matrix.title: AIR → DBR mapping
matrix.subtitle: All currently supported AIR lines and their pinned DBR LTS

conflicts: Prior bot answers disagree on whether an older AIR line still maps to DBR 11.3-LTS. Prefer the more recent first-hand source per 020-context-accuracy.mdc; if unresolvable, mark ⚠️ unverified and lower confidence.

confidence.verified: AIR → DBR mapping for 3.x / 2.x / 1.x / 0.13.x corroborated by the Confluence version matrix and a #nextgen-release announcement.
confidence.not_verified: The DBR 11.3-LTS mapping and the exact ECR-tag retention count. Code-side check on ng-abacus-insights-runtime not required for a version-mapping question.

for_you.text: Self-service: open Databricks → Compute → Cluster Policies. The DBR is encoded in the policy tag (e.g. AIR:3.0.16 - 17.3.x-scala2.13 - Pipeline).
Recommendation: if you are on AIR 0.13.x (DBR 13.3-LTS, EoS Aug 22 2026), schedule an upgrade to AIR 3.x now — DBR 13.3 support ends within a month.
